questions.question: A beaker contains acid and water in the ratio 1 : x. When 300 ml of the mixture and 150 ml of water are mixed, the ratio of acid and water becomes 2 : 5. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: In 300 ml of mixture Volume of acid =$$\frac{1}{1+x}\times\ 300$$ and volume of water = $$\frac{x}{1+x}\times\ 300$$ Now as per condition $$\frac{300}{1+x}:\frac{300x+150}{1+x}=2:5$$ solving we get 1500 =600x+300 600x=1200 x=2

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A mixture is composed of 11 parts of pure milk and 2 parts of water. If 35 litres of water were added to the mixture then the new mixture will contain twice as much pure milk as water, then how many litres of pure milk does the original mixture contain?
questions.explanation: Let pure milk be 11x Then water will be 2x Now when 35 liters of water is added we get water = 2x+35 Now as per condition 2(2x+35) =11x 4x+70 =11x 7x=70 x=10 So originial amount of pure milk = 10(11) =110

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A starts a taxi service by investing Rs 25 lakhs. After 3 months, B joins the business by investing Rs 40 lakhs then 4 months after B joined, C too joins them by investing Rs 50 lakhs. One year after A started the business they make Rs 2,73,000 in profit. What is C's share of the profit (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: Ratio= $$25\times12 months:40\times9 months:50\times5 months$$ C's share of the profit (in Rs)= $$(50\times5\times273000)\div(25\times12+40\times9+50\times5)$$= Rs.75000 Therefore, Option C is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A, B and C invest in a business in the ratio 4 : 5 : 7. C is a sleeping partner, so his share of profits will be half of what it would have been if he were a working partner. If they make Rs 36,000 profit of which 25% is reinvested in the business, how much does B get (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: Now Ratio is 4:5:7 Now as per given the C's share is half of what it should be so we get ratio as 4:5:3.5=8:10:7 Now The actual profit = 75% of 36000 =27000 Now therefore share of B = $$\frac{10}{25}\times\ 27000=10,800$$

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A can do a work in 36 days and B in 12 days. If they work on it together for 3 days, then what fraction of work is left?
questions.explanation: A & B total working unit is LCM of 36 & 12= 36 units. A's working unit in a day=$$36\div36$$= 1 unit B's working unit in a day=$$36\div12$$= 3 unit Total work done by both in 3days= $$(1+3)\times3$$= 12 units. Remaining work= (36-12)units= 24 units. Fraction of work left= $$24\div36$$= $$2\div3$$ Therefore, Option A is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A can paint a house in 45 days and B can do it in 15 days. Along with C, they did the job in 5 days only. Then, C alone can do the job in how many days?
questions.explanation: Let the total work be 45 units and work by A be 1unit/day Now work by B will be 45/15 = 3 units per day Now ABC together complete the work in 5 days so their 1 day work = 9units so C's one day work =9-1-3 =5 Now therefore C completes work in 45/5 = 9 days

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A,B and C together can finish a task in 7.5 days. C is thrice as productive as A and B alone can do the task in 15 days. In how many days can A and C do the job if B goes on leave?
questions.explanation: Let work done by B be a units per day Now therefore total work = 15a Now ABC does the work in 7.5 days so thet do 15a/7.5 = 2a units of work per day Now B does a units of work in a day so A and C does a units of work in 1 day Now total work =15a A and C together does a units in 1 day so they will complete 15a units of work in 15a/a = 15 days

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A, B and C can do job in 9, 12 and 36 days respectively if they worked alone. A leaves after they have worked together for 3 days. In how many days can B and C do the rest of the job?
questions.explanation: A, B, C working together units is LCM of 9, 12, 36= 36 units A can work $$36\div9$$= 4 units in a day. B can work $$36\div12$$= 3 units in a day. C can work $$36\div36$$= 1 units in a day. A, B, C 3 days work= $$4\times3+3\times3+1\times3$$= 24 units. Remaining work is to be done by B & C= (36-24)units= 12 units. Total days taken by B & C together to complete the work after A leaves= $$12\div(3+1)$$= 3 days. Therefore, Option A is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Giving two successive discounts of 40% is equal to giving one discount of ________%.
questions.explanation: Successive discount= $$2x-(x^{2}\div100)$$ So, by using above formula, putting x= 40, we get, Successive discount= 64% Therefor, Option C is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If a website is selling smartphone at Rs 18,000 which is marked at Rs 25,000, then what is the discount (in %) at which the smartphone is being sold?
questions.explanation: Discount in price of smartphone= Rs.(25000-18000)= Rs.7000 % discount given= $$7000\times100\div25000$$= 28% Therefore, Option C is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If on an item there is 12% discount on the marked price of Rs 10,000 but the item is sold at Rs 8,360 only then what additional discount (in %) did the customer get?
questions.explanation: CP after first discount= (100-12)% of Rs.10,000= Rs.8800 But the item is sold at Rs 8,360, So, additional discount offered= $$(8800-8360)\times100\div8800$$= 5% Therefore, Option C is cprrect.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A shopkeeper marks up his wares by 125% and offers 25% discount. What will be the selling price if the cost price (in Rs) is Rs 640?
questions.explanation: Marked Price= (100+125)%= 225% of Rs.640= Rs.1440 Discount offered= 25% So, Selling Price= (100-25)% of Rs.1440= Rs.1080 Therefore, Option A is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Priya's marks in History and Geography are in the ratio 5 : 7. If she got 14 marks more in Geography than in History, what are her History marks?
questions.explanation: Assuming, marks in History= x and in Geography= y Ratio of their marks= $$x\div y=5\div7$$ .....(i) Since she got 14 marks more in Geography than in History, So, (y-14)= x .....(ii) Solving equation (i) & (ii) we get, x= 35 and y= 49 Therefore, Option D is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: The ratio of present ages of Rahul and his sister is 3 : 4. Before 10 years the ratio of their ages was 13:19. What is Rahul's present age (in years)?
questions.explanation: Assuming, present age of Rahul= x years and his sister= y years. So, Present ratio= $$x\div y= 3\div4$$      .....(i) Before 10 years the ratio of their ages =$$(x-10)\div (y-10)= 13\div19$$          ......(ii) Solving equation (i) & (ii), We get, x=36 years and y=48 years. Therefore, Option A is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: What is the third proportional to 9 and 15?
questions.explanation: Let the third proportional be x. 9:15::15:x $$9\div15=15\div x$$ Solving we get x= 25 Therefore, Option D is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: According to the will, the wealth of Rs 11,50,000 was to be divided between the son and the daughter in the ratio $$\frac{2}{3} : \frac{5}{4}$$. How much share did the son get (in Rs lakhs)?
questions.explanation: LCM of the denominators i.e 3,4=12 Therefore, New ratio= $$\frac{2}{3}\times12 : \frac{5}{4}\times12$$= 8:15 So, B's share= $$8\div(8+15)\times1150000$$= Rs. 4 lakhs. Therefore, Option D is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If Rs 7,800 is to be divided between A, B and C in the ratio $$\frac{1}{2} : \frac{1}{3} : \frac{1}{4}$$, then how much share will B get (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: LCM of the denominators of $$\frac{1}{2} : \frac{1}{3} : \frac{1}{4}$$ i.e. LCM of 2,3,4= 12 Therefore, new ratio=$$\frac{1}{2}\times12 : \frac{1}{3}\times12 : \frac{1}{4}\times12$$ = 6:4:3 So, B's share= $$4\div(6+4+3)\times7800$$= 2400. Therefore, Option C is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Bunty had candies and chewing gums in his sweet box in the ratio 7 : 13. After he has eaten 8 candies and 11 chewing gums the ratio became 1 : 2. How many candies does he have now?
questions.explanation: Assuming, number of candies= x & number of chewing gums= y Initial ratio= $$x\div y$$ =$$7\div13$$. Therefore, $$x=(7\times y)\div13$$        .....(i) After he has eaten 8 candies and 11 chewing gums, Ratio= $$(x-8)\div(y-11)=1\div2$$         ......(ii) Putting the value x in equation (ii), We get, x=35 & y=65. Candies he has now= 35-8= 27. Therefore, Option D is correct.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: The average weight of P, Q and R is 71 kg. If the average weight of P and Q be 66 kg and that of Q and R be 76.5 kg, then the weight (in kg) of Q is.
questions.explanation: Total Weight of P,Q & R= $$(P+Q+R)\div3=71$$ Therefore,P+Q+R= 213        ......(i) Total weight of P & Q= $$(P+Q)\div2=66$$ Therefore, P+Q= 132        .......(ii) Total weight of Q & R= $$(Q+R)\div2=76.5$$ Therefore,Q+R= 153         .......(iii) Solving equation (i) & (ii), we get, R= 81 Solving equation (i) & (iii), we get, P= 60 Putting the value of P & R in equation (i), We get, Q= 72. Therefore, Option B is the correct answer.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Price of petrol increased from Rs 60/liter to Rs 75/liter. How much should the consumption of petrol be reduced (in %) so as to increase expenditure by only 10%?
questions.explanation: Now price of petrol = 60 Let the consumption be 10 liters So expenditure = 600 Now Expenditure is increased by 10% so the expenditure = 600(1.1) =660 Now the price = 75 per liter so consumption will be : 660/75 =8.8 liters Now therefore the percentage reduction = $$\frac{\left(10-8.8\right)}{10}\times\ 100=12$$

questions.question: P: The main cause of global warming is the human being's careless activities. Q: There are many causes of the global warming which affects human lives and health in many aspects. R: Fossil fuels burning for many reasons are highly responsible to increase global warming. S: Ever increasing human population is demanding more resources to live easy and healthy life such as land to live and for crop cultivation, technological growth for comfort and to deal with increasing temperature of atmosphere, etc.

questions.subject: english
questions.question: P: We need to be more physically healthy and fit to win the current environmental challenges. Q: It keeps us away from the diseases, infections and other lethal diseases by reducing extra kilos, improving the strength of muscle and bones, increasing immunity level, and maintaining the healthy functioning of our major body organs. R: In the modern time, where pollution, global warming and other environmental issues have covered us, the importance of health and fitness has increased to a great extent. S: It helps us to look better with lots of patience and confidence level.

questions.subject: english
questions.question: P: All these factors together can help eradicate poverty, crimes and disease rates. Q: Girls’ education is important from every perspective of life and society. R: Not only do they take right decisions for themselves, but they also provide better education and health care options to their children. S: Educated women lead a healthier life compared to the uneducated women; they participate in the family matters and in the formal labour markets too; earn well, marry at a considerable age and plan a family in a better manner.

S: Forests are beautiful natural settings where we can go and experience nature in its splendid expression.

S: We can be kind to our family members, relatives, friends and neighbours.

R: If we waste our time on vain activities we either get stressed out doing the tasks we need to do or fail to do what needs to be done. S: We all have the same number of hours at our disposal each day.

questions.question: What is the sum of first 40 terms of 1 + 3 + 4 + 5 + 7 + 7 + 10 + 9 + ….?
questions.explanation: The sequence has to be split into two with alternate terms: S1=1+4+7+10+…(S1=1+4+7+10+…(AP with A1=1,D1=3 S2=3+5+7+9+...(S2=3+5+7+9+...(AP with A2=3,D2=2) Sum of 40 terms of the original series = Sum of 20 terms of S1+ Sum of 20 terms of S2 S=N[2A1+(N−1)D1]/2+N[2A2+(N−1)D2]/2 ⟹S=[20∗((2∗1)+(19∗3))]+[20∗((2∗3)+(19∗2)]/2 ⟹S=10∗[59+44]=1030 1      1       1

questions.question: How many employees of department A and C together are living in city Z?
questions.explanation: Total number of employees = 80,000 Now percentage of employees in department A = 10% so number = 800 Now Number of employees living in city Z from department A = 10 × 8000 = 800 Now number of employees in department C = 100 × 80000 = 9600 Now number of employees in city Z from department C = 6 × 9600 = 8000 So total we get 8000+800 = 8800

questions.explanation: Total number of employees = 80,000 Now percentage of employees in department A = 10% so number = 8000 Now Number of employees living in city Z from department A = 10 × 8000 = 800 Now employees in department E = 80, 000 × 100 = 28, 800 Now male employees working in department E = 9 × 28, 800 = 25, 600 Now percentage of male employees working in department E with respect to employees living in city Z from department A will be k 800 × 100 = 25, 600 we get k = 3200

questions.question: A and B invest in a business in the ratio 2 : 5. If 50% of the total profit goes to charity and A's share is Rs 3.6 lakhs, the total profit is Rs __________ lakhs.
questions.explanation: Let the total profit be P Now Part going to charity = 0.5P Now part remaining = 0.5P Now the profit is always divided in the ratio of investment so 0.5P will be divided between A and B in the ratio 2:5 so A's share = 7 × 0.5P =P/7 Now P/7 = 3.6 we get P = 25.2 lakhs

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A is thrice as productive as C. Together they can complete a job in 22.5 days. If B joins them after they have worked for 15 days then in how many days can they finish the rest of the job if B alone can do the job in 15 days?
questions.explanation: Let the work done by C be x units/day Therefore work done by A will be 3x units/day Now therefore total work per day = 4x units Now total work done = 22.5(4x) =90x units/day Now B alone does the work in 15 days so B's per day work = 6x units/ day Now for 15 days A and C worked so work completed in 15 days = 60x Now remaining work = 30x Now time taken by A,B,C together to complete 30x units of work : 30x/10x = 3 days

questions.question: What is the Number of candidates who had applied if the ratio of selected to unselected was 14 : 25. If 35 less had applied and 10 less selected, the ratio of selected to unselected would have been 3 : 5?
questions.explanation: Let number of selected candidates be 14x Now number of unselected candidates will be : 25x Now total applied = 39x ...(1) Now if 35 less applied we get applied = 39x-35       (2) If 10 less got selected then we get selected as 14x-10 Now therefore unselected = 25x-25 Now by condition : 14x-10 : 25x-25 =3:5 solving we get 70x-50 = 75x-75 we get 5x=25 x=5 So candidates applied = 39x = 195

questions.explanation: Let cost price of goat be x and cost price of sheep be y Now by condition we get: x+y =14,250 (1) Now selling price of goat =0.8x And selling price of sheep =1.1y Now by condition we get 1.1y=0.8x 0.8x-1.1y =0 Solving both we get y=6000, x=8250 So the cost price of cheaper animal is 6000

Now as 4-2 =5-3 =6-4 =7-5 =2 M will be of the form :k LCM(4,5,6,7) -2=420k-2 M will be 418,838,1258.... Now the largest 4 digit number of the series will be : 418+(n-1)*422<10,000 so we get                                           418+420(22) =9658 Now 9658 mod 9 = 28 mod 9 =1

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: The ratio of curved surface area and volume of a cylinder is 1 : 7. The ratio of total surface area and volume is 187 : 770. What is the respective ratio of its base radius and height?
questions.explanation: We have : 2π rh : π r2 h = 1 : 7 we get 2:r = 1:7 (1) r= 14 From 2nd condition we get : 2π r (r + h) : π r2 h = 187 : 770 (2) Substituting r=14 in (2) we get h =20 Therefore r:h = 7:10

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A hemisphere is kept on top of a cube. Its front view is shown in the given figure. The total height of the figure is 21 cm. The ratio of curved surface area of hemisphere and total surface area of cube is 11 : 42. What is the total volume (in cm3 ) of figure?

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A regular pyramid has a square base. The height of the pyramid is 22 cm and side of its base is 14 cm. Volume of pyramid is equal to the volume of a sphere. What is the radius (in cm) of the sphere?
questions.explanation: Volume of pyramid= (1/3)b2 h =(1/3) × 142 × 22=4312/3 . Volume of sphere = (4/3)π × r3 So, (4/3)πr3 = 4312/3 or, r3 = 343 or, r=7 . B is correct choice. [ sin(y−z)+sin(y+z)+2 sin y]

questions.question: If sec θ(cos θ + sin θ) = √2, then what is the value of (cos θ−sin θ) ?

questions.question: What is the value of sin4 (90−θ) + [cos2 (90−θ)]−1 ?
questions.explanation: 1               1 sin4 (90−θ) + [cos2 (90−θ)]−1 . 1                        1 = cos4 θ − 1−sin2 θ . 1                    1 = cos4 θ − cos2 θ . 1−cos2 θ =     cos4 θ . sin2 θ = cos4 θ . = tan2 θ sec2 θ . A is correct choice. [tan(90−A)+cot(90−A)] 2

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: What is the value of {sin(90 + x) cos[π − (x − y)]} + { cos(90 + x) sin[π − (x − y)]}?
questions.explanation: We know that : Sin (A + B) = SinACosB + CosASinB . So, {sin(90 + x) cos[π − (x − y)]} + {cos(90 + x) sin[π − (x − y)]} = Sin ((90∘ + x) + π − (x − y)) . = Sin (90∘ + x + 180∘ − x + y) . = Sin (270∘ + y) . = −Cos y . A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: The angle of elevation of an aeroplane from a point on the ground is 60°. After flying for 30 seconds, the angle of elevation changes to 30°. If the aeroplane is flying at a height of 4500 m, then what is the speed (in m/s) of aeroplane?

So, AB = CD = (P C − P D) = 4500               3 − 1500 3 = 3000 3 m. 3000 3 m              m So, Speed of plane=       30   sec = 100    3 sec . B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A kite is flying in the sky. The length of string between a point on the ground and kite is 420 m. The angle of elevation of string with the ground is 30°. Assuming that there is no slack in the string, then what is the height (in metres) of the kite?
questions.explanation: PM       PM So, tan 30∘ = PQ =              x . x or, P M =       3 . x       2 So, x2 + ( 3 )            = 4202 . or, x2 = 4202 × 4 . or, x = 363.7306 . x    363.7306 So, P M =       3 =     3    = 210 . A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A balloon leaves from a point P rises at a uniform speed. After 6 minutes, an observer situated at a distance of 450√3 metres from point P observes that angle of elevation of the balloon is 60°. Assume that point of observation and point P are on the same level. What is the speed (in m/s) of the balloon?
questions.explanation: PB So, 450 3 = tan 60∘ . or, P B = 1350 meter . 1350 meter      meter So, Speed of balloon = 60×6     sec = 3.75 sec . B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: What is the total number of wooden bats of brand A manufactured by company T?
questions.explanation: the total number of wooden bats of brand A manufactured by company T is = 25 × 0.55 × 550000 = 72600 . D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: N = Wooden bats of Brand B manufactured by U. M = Total wooden bats manufactured by R and W together. N What is the value of M ?

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: P = Sum of wooden bats of Brand B manufactured by S and wooden bats of Brand A manufactured by W. Q = Difference of Brand B wooden bats and Brand A wooden bats manufactured by U. What is the value P - Q?

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Taking all 6 companies together, how many wooden bats of Brand A have been produced?
questions.explanation: Total number of wooden bats produced by all the companies together 21               8          6       41           7           5 = 550000 ( 25 × 0.45 + 17 × 0.3 + 25 × 0.55 + 55 × 0.25 + 22 × 0.4 + 11 × 0.6) =691000 . A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: X = Average of plastic bats manufactured by V, U and T. Y = Wooden bats of Brand A manufactured by V. What is the value X - Y?

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A drum contains 80 litres of ethanol. 20 litres of this liquid is removed and replaced with water. 20 litres of this mixture is again removed and replaced with water. How much water (in litres) is present in this drum now?
questions.explanation: We know the formula: X = A(1 - R/C)n Here X = Liquid remaining after replacement A = Total quantity of liquid before replacement R = Quantity of replaced liquid C = Total Capacity of drum n = No. of times the liquid was replaced ⇒ A = 80, R = 20, C = 80 and n = 2 Putting these values in the formula, ⇒ X = 80 × (1 - 1/4) 2 ⇒ X = 80 × 9/16 ⇒ X = 45 litres ⇒ Amount of ethanol present after replacement = 45 litres ∴ Amount of water present after replacement = 80 - 45 = 35 litres C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: An alloy is made by mixing metal A costing Rs 2000/kg and metal B costing Rs 400/kg in the ratio A:B = 3:1. What is the cost (in Rs) of 8 kilograms of this alloy?
questions.explanation: Since Ratio in which A and B are mixed = 3 : 1 ⇒ Weight of metal A in 8 kg of alloy = 3/4 × 8 = 6 kg ⇒ Weight of metal B in 8 kg of alloy = 1/4 × 8 = 2 kg ∴ Cost of 8 kg of the alloy = 2000 × 6 + 400 × 2 = Rs. 12800 D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A, B and C invest to start a restaurant. The total investment was Rs 3 lakhs. B invested Rs 50,000 more than A and C invested Rs 25,000 less than B. If the profit at the end of the year was Rs 14,400 then what is C's share of the profit (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: According to question : B = A + 50000 and C = B − 25000 . or, C = A + 25000 According to question : A + (A + 50000) + (A + 25000) = 300000 . or, 3A = 225000 . or, A = 75000 . So, B = 125000 and C = 100000 . So, A's Profit:B's Profit :C's Profit = 75000 : 125000 : 100000 = 75:125:100= 3:5:4 . So, C will get = 14400 × 12 = 4800 Rs . B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Two businessmen A and B invest in a business in the ratio 5 : 8. They decided to reinvest 30% of the profit they earned back into the business. The remaining profit they distributed amongst themselves. If A's share of the profit was Rs 87,500 then how much profit (in Rs) did the business make?
questions.explanation: Since Ratio of investment = 5 : 8, ⇒ Ratio of profit distribution will also be = 5 : 8 Let us take the total profit = P Since they decided to reinvest the 30% profit so remaining profit that is been distributed = 0.7P ⇒ A's share in the profit = 5/13 × 0.7P ⇒ 7P/26 = 87500 ∴ P = Rs. 325000 D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Working alone A can do the task in 27 hours and B can do it in 54 hours. Find C's share (in Rs) if A, B and C get paid Rs 4,320 for completing a task in 12 hours on which they worked together.
questions.explanation: Let say, C completes the task in c hrs . 1         1   1   1 So, 27 + 54 + c = 12 . 1         1       1   1       27−12−6   1 or, c = 12 − 27 − 54 =              27×12 = 36 . So, c=36 . 1   1   1 A's share:B's share:C's share = 27 : 54 : 36 = 4 : 2 : 3 . So, C will get = 4320 × 9 = 1440 . A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If A had worked alone he would have taken 63 hours to do the task. What is B's share, if A and B work together on a task finishing it in 36 hours and they get paid Rs 5,950 for it?
questions.explanation: Let say, B takes b hrs to complete the task . So, According to question : 1    1    1 b + 63 = 36 . 1        1   1        7−4       1 or, b = 36 − 63 = 4×7×9 = 84 . or, b=84 . So, B and A will take their shares in 63 : 84 = 3 : 4 ratio . So, B's share = 5950 × 7 = 2550 . C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Working together A, B and C can complete a task in 12 days. A and B can do the task in 55 days and 66 days respectively if they worked alone. In how many days can C do the task if he worked alone?
questions.explanation: Let say, C alone take c days to complete the work . 1       1   1    1 So, c + 55 + 66 = 12 . 1        1   1    1         55−12−10    33    1 or, c = 12 − 55 − 66 =              660   = 660 = 20 . or, c = 20 . C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: B would have taken 10 hours more than what A would have taken to complete a task if each of them worked alone. Working together they can complete the task in 12 hours. How many hours would B take to do 50% of the task?
questions.explanation: Let say, A took x hrs to complete the task . So, B will take (x+10) hrs to complete it . So, According to question : 1    1      1 x + x+10 = 12 . or, 12 (x + x + 10) = x2 + 10x . or, x2 − 14x − 120 = 0 . or, x2 − 20x + 14x − 120 = 0 . or, (x − 20) (x + 14) = 0 . So, either x = 20 or x = −14 . So, x should be 20 . So, B takes 30 hrs to complete the work . So, B will take 15 hrs to complete the 50% of work . B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Giving two successive discounts of 20% is same as giving one discount of ________ %.
questions.explanation: Two successive discount of 20% gives you to sell the thing on 0.80 × 0.80 = 0.64 or 64% of its cost price. So, resultant discount is = (1 − 0.64) or 36% . A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A retailer marks up his goods by 150% and offers 40% discount. What will be the selling price (in Rs) if the cost price is Rs 800?
questions.explanation: Resultant selling price = 800 × (1 + 100 ) × 0.60 = 1200 Rs. A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: On a television of brand A the discount is 25% and on television of brand B the discount is 40%. The price of B after discount Rs 2,250 greater than the price of A after discount. What is the marked price of A (in Rs) if marked price of B is Rs 35,000?
questions.explanation: Let say, marked price on A is m. According to question : 0.75m + 2250 = 0.60 × 35000 . or, m = 0.75         = 25000 . C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If 60% discount is offered on the marked price and selling price becomes equal to cost price then what was the % mark up?
questions.explanation: Let say cost price is c and marked price is m. So, 0.40m=c. or, m=2.5c. so, he marked up by 1.5 times more than of cost price or by 150% more than cost price. C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If 3A = 6B = 9C; What is A : B : C
questions.explanation: Let say,3A=6B=9C=k . so, A:B:C=(k/3:k/6:k/9)=6:3:2. B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: How many job applicants had applied if the ratio of selected to unselected was 19:17. If 1,200 less had applied and 800 less selected, then the ratio of selected to unselected would have been 1:1.
questions.explanation: Let s and u be the no. of candidates selected and unselected respectively. Total candidates applied = s+u Condition 1: Ratio of selected to unselected was 19: 17. s/u = (19/17) 17s= 19u ——(1) Condition 2: If 1200 less had applied and 800 less selected, the ratio of selected to unselected would have been 1 : 1 Total candidates applied = s+u-1200 Selected candidates = s-800 Then, unselected candidates = s+u-1200 - (s-800) = s+u-1200-s+800 = u-400 (s-800)/(u-400) = (1/1) s-800 = u-400 —-(2) From (1), u = (17/19)s Substitute in (2), s-800 = (17/19)s -400 s= 3800 u = 3400 s+u = 3800 + 3400 = 7200 The number of applicants is 7200 B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: What is the third proportional to 10 and 20?
questions.explanation: The third proportional of two numbers p and q is defined to be that number r such that p : q = q : r. Here, required third proportional of 10 & 20, and let it be 'a' => 10 : 20 = 20 : a 10a = 20 x 20 => a = 40 D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: The ratio of the sum of the salaries of A and B to the difference of their salaries is 11:1 and the ratio of the sum of the salaries of B and C to the difference of their salaries is also 11:1. If A's salary is the highest and C's is the lowest then what is B's salary (in Rs) given the total of all their salaries is Rs 1,82,000?
questions.explanation: Let the salaries of A, B and C are A, B and C respectively. ⇒ (A+B)/(A-B)=11/1. And, (B+C)/(B-C)=11/1. Applying componendo and dividendo, ⇒ A : B : C = 36 : 30 : 25 Let A = 36x, B = 30x and C = 25x Given A + B + C = 182000 ⇒ 91x = 182000 ⇒ x = 2000 ∴ B = 30 × 2000 = Rs. 60000 B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If by increasing the price of a ticket in the ratio 8:11 the number of tickets sold fall in the ratio 23:21 then what is the increase (in Rs) in revenue if revenue before increase in price of ticket was Rs 36,800?
questions.explanation: Let say ,price of tickets are 8x and 11x And, sells of tickets are 23y and 21y . So, before change revenue=23y×8x. So, (8x×23y)=36800 or, xy=200. So, new revenue=11×21×200=46200. So, increase in revenue=46200-36800=9400. B is correct choice

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: The ratio of ages of the father and mother was 11:10 when their son was born. The ratio of ages of the father and mother will be 19:18 when the son will be twice his present age. What is the ratio of present ages of father and mother?
questions.explanation: Ratio of parents present age is 11:10 And the ratio of ages of the father and mother will be 19:18 when the son will be twice his present age Let the present age of son be x Then the twice age will be 2x According to question: 11+2x   19 10+2x = 18 18(11 + 2x) = 19(10 + 2x) 198 + 36x = 190 + 38x 8 = 2x x=4 Ratio of present ages will be: 11+x   11+4 10+x = 10+4 Required ratio is: (15/14). A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Of the 3 numbers whose average is 22, the first is 8 th the sum of other 2. What is the first number?
questions.explanation: Let say, first number is x and other two numbers are y and z. So, (x+y+z)=66. And, x=(3/8)(y+z) or, (y+z)=8x/3 . So, x+(8x/3)=66. or, x=(66×3)/11=18. D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: The average of three consecutive odd numbers is 52 more than 3 rd of the largest of these numbers. What is the smallest of these numbers?
questions.explanation: Let say, numbers are x,x+2 and x+4. According to question : (3x+6)/3=52+(x+4)/3 . or, 3x+6=156+x+4. or, 2x=154. or, x=77. So,D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A batsman scores 98 runs in the 17th match of his career. His average runs per match increased by 2.5. What is his average before the 17th match?
questions.explanation: Let say, average of first 16 match was x. So, first 17 matches average score is =(16x+98)/17. According to question: (16x+98)/17=x+2.5 or, 16x+98=17x+42.5 or,x=55.5 . D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: What is the average of all numbers between 100 and 200 which are divisible by 13?
questions.explanation: There are 8 numbers present between 100 and 200 that are divisible by 13 ,and those are : 104,117,...,182,195. So,total of them=(8/2)(104+195)=1196. So, average of them=1196/8=149.5. D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A vendor buys bananas at 9 for Rs 8 and sells at 8 for Rs 9. What will be the profit or loss (in %)?
questions.explanation: C.P for 1 banana is =8/9 Rs. S.P for 1 banana is =9/8 Rs. So, profit is=(9/8-8/9)/(8/9)×100=26.56%. C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If a stall sells a pizza at Rs 200 he makes 20% loss if he wants to make 10% profit then at what price (in Rs) should he sell?
questions.explanation: According to question: 0.80x=200 or, x=250. So, to make a profit of 10% ,he has to sell it for (250×1.1)=275 Rs. C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A wholesaler had 200 dozens of mangoes. He sold some of these mangoes at 20% profit and the rest at 10% profit, so that he made 13% profit on selling all the mangoes. How many mangoes (in dozens) did he sell at 20% profit?
questions.explanation: Let the mangoes at profit 20% be x dozens ⇒ Mangoes at profit 10% = (200 - x) dozens ⇒ 1.2x + 1.1(200 - x) = 200 × 1.13 ⇒ 0.1x = 6 ⇒ x = 60 ∴ Number of mangoes sold at 20% profit = 60 dozens. B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If the selling price is tripled and cost price doubled the profit would become 65%. What is the present profit (in %)?
questions.explanation: Let say , selling price is s and cost price is c. So,profit= ((s-c)/c)×100. New profit=Let say , selling price is s and cost price is c. So,profit= ((s-c)/c)×100. New profit=((3s-2c)/2c)×100=65. or, 3s/2c=1.65 or,s/c=1.1 . present profit=(1.1-1)×100=10% . D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Two numbers are 90% and 75% lesser than a third number. By what % should the first number be increased so that it becomes equal to the second number?
questions.explanation: Let say, third number is x. So, first and second number is 0.10x and 0.25x . So, first number shall be increased by ((0.25x-0.10x)/0.10x)×100=150% . C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: When a number is increased by 216, it becomes 140% of itself. What is the number?
questions.explanation: According to question: 1.4x=x+216 or, x=216/0.40=540. A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A man donates 30% of his wealth to charity. 30% and 25% of the remaining wealth to his wife and son respectively. The rest he divides equally between his three daughters. One of his daughter gets Rs 42 lakh as her share. What was the man's wealth (in Rs lakhs)?
questions.explanation: Let say ,his total wealth is x lakh . After giving to charity,he left with 0.70x lakh. After giving to his wife and daughter,he left with=(1-0.30-0.25)0.70x=0.315x. According to question: 0.315x/3=42 or, x=42/0.105=400. B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A bus travels 720 km in 20 hours. Calculate its average speed in meters/second.
questions.explanation: Average speed is=(720×1000)/(3600×20) m/s . = 10 m/s . D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If a boat goes upstream at a speed of 21 km/h and comes back the same distance at 28 km/h. What is the average speed (in km/hr) for the total journey?
questions.explanation: Let say total distance is d km. So, average speed=2d/(d/21+d/28) km/hr. =(2d×84)/(4d+3d) km/hr =24 km/hr. B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Two runners A and B start running at 12 km/hr and 16 km/hr towards each other. They meet after 1 hour and 30 minutes. How far (in km) were they from each other when they started?
questions.explanation: Distance between A and B =1.5(12+16) km =42 km . A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Flight A usually takes 1 hour more than Flight B to travel a distance of 7200 km. Due to engine trouble speed of flight B falls by a factor of 6 th , so it takes 36 minutes more than Flight A to complete the same journey? What is the speed of Flight A (in km/hr)?
questions.explanation: Let say, B's speed is x km/h. So, B takes(7200/x) hr . So, A takes ((7200/x)+1) hr. B's new speed is (5x/6) km/h . So, B's new time=(7200×6)/5x hr . So, 43200/5x = ((7200/x) + 1) + 0.6 or,7200/5x=1.6 or, x=900. A take (7200/900+1)=9 hr. So,A's speed=7200/9=800 km/hr. A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: In how many years will Rs 2,000 yield Rs 662 as compound interest at 10% per annum compounded annually?
questions.explanation: Let say,it will take n number of years. So, 2000(1 + 10/100)n − 2000 = 662 or, (1.1)n = 2662/2000 = 1.331 or, (1.1)n = (1.1)3 or, n=3 A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: What is the compound interest earned on Rs 80,000 at 40% per annum in 1 year compounded quarterly?

questions.question: An investor invested his saving in the stock market. The value of his investments increased by 12% and 9% in the first year and the second year respectively. If the value of his investments after two years became Rs 97,664 then how much had he invested (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: He has invested=97664/(1.12×1.09) Rs. =80000 Rs. C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: What is the rate of interest (in %) if simple interest earned on a certain sum for the 3 years is Rs 6,000 and compound interest earned for 2 years is Rs 4,160?
questions.explanation: According to question: Pr/100=6000/3=2000. Again, P (1 + r/100)2 − P = 4160 or, P + 2P r/100 + P r2 /1002 − P = 4160 or, 4000 + 2000r/100 = 4160 or, r=8%. B is correct choice.

questions.question: If a + b + c = 9, ab + bc + ca = 26, a 3 + b3 = 91, b3 + c3 = 72 and c3 + a 3 = 35, then what is the value of abc?
questions.explanation: We know that : a 3 + b3 + c3 = (a + b + c) (a 2 + b2 + c2 − ab − ac − bc) + 3abc . Here, a + b + c = 9, ab + bc + ca = 26, a 3 + b3 = 91, b3 + c3 = 72 and c3 + a 3 = 35 So, 2 (a 3 + b3 + c3 ) = 72 + 91 + 35 = 198. So, a 3 + b3 + c3 = 99 . And , a 2 + b2 + c2 = 92 − 2 × 26 = 29 . So, a 3 + b3 + c3 = (a + b + c) (a 2 + b2 + c2 − ab − ac − bc) + 3abc or, 99 = (9) (29 − 26) + 3abc . or, abc = 24 . B is correct choice.

questions.explanation: Side of square is 20 cm. So, diagonal of square is 20√2 cm. So,to calculate perimeter of triangle,we get to count each diagonal 2 times and 4 sides only one time . So, required perimeter=4×20+4×20√2 =(80+80√2) cm. B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If ABCDEF is a regular hexagon, then what is the value (in degrees) of ∠AEB?
questions.explanation: Sum of angles of a polygon = (n - 2)180 n = number of sides Sum of angles of a hexagon is 720°, since it is a regular hexagon each interior angle has measure 120° Quadrilateral ABEF contains half the angle measure of the hexagon; its interior angle measure = 360° angle AFE = 120° (angle of hexagon ABCDEF) triangle AFE is isosceles; angle FAE = angle FEA = 30° sum of angles of triangle = 180° angle FAE + angle EAB = 120°; therefor angle EAB = 90° angle ABE is one half of 120° = 60° angle ABE + angle EAB + angle AEB = 180° therfor angle AEB = 30° B is correct choice.

questions.explanation: Triangle CDE is equilateral triangle. So,CE=CD=BC . So, For triangle BCE, CE=BC . So, triangle BCE is a isosceles triangle . So, angle CBE=angle CEB. Now,angle BCD=90° and angle ECD=60°. So, angle BCE=90°+60°=150°. So, 2× angle BEC=180°-150°=30°. So, angle BEC= 15°. A is correct choice.

questions.question: What is the difference between the number of students who were present in Physics and Biology examination from school N?
questions.explanation: Difference of students in Physics and Biology in N= 0.35     × 0.65 × (0.58 − 0.42) = 18075 . C is correct choice.

questions.explanation: ( 0.6 ×0.4×0.7 ) Required Percentage = (113520+101520+24003 ) × 100 = 22.16 %. E is correct choice.

So, difference in Present in Physics and Biology in K= 154700 × (0.68 − 0.32) = 55692. So, Present in physics in A= 55692 × 2.5 = 139230. Total students present in L=      0.40 × 0.6 = 152280 . 152280     1692 So, required ratio = 139230 = 1547 . B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A jar contains a blend of a fruit juice and water in the ratio 5 : x. When 1 litre of water is added to 4 litres of the blend the ratio of fruit juice to water becomes 1 : 1. What is the value of x?
questions.explanation: In 4 ltr : 5       20 Fruit juice =4 × 5+x = 5+x . x    4x And, Water = 4 × 5+x = 5+x . According to question , 5+x 4                 1 x 5+x+1      =1. 20          4x or, 5+x = 5+x + 1 . or, 20 − x = 5 + 4x . or, x = 5         =3. So, A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: An alloy contains copper and tin in the ratio 3 : 2. If 250 gm of copper is added to this alloy then the copper in it becomes double the quantity of tin in it. What is the amount (in gm) of tin in the alloy?
questions.explanation: Let say, copper and tin are 3k and 2k. So, according to question, 3k+250=2×2k. or, k=250. So, tin was =500 gm. D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A starts a cement trading business by investing Rs 5 lakhs. After 2 months, B joins the business by investing Rs 10 lakhs and then 4 months after B joined C too joins them by investing Rs 20 lakhs. 1 year after A started the business they make Rs 3,50,000 in profit. What is B's share of the profit (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: Ratio of profit of A,B and C =(5×12:10×10:20×6)=(60:100:120) =3:5:6. B will get=(5×350000/14)=125000 B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A, B and C invest in a business in the ratio 3 : 6 : 5. A and C are working partners. Only B is a sleeping partner hence his share will be 4 th of what it would have been if he were a working partner. If they make Rs 50,000 profit, half of which is reinvested in the business and the other half is distributed between the partners, then how much does C get (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: Original ratio of their profit is =(3:6×3/4:5) =(12:18:20)=(6:9:10). So, C will get =25000×10/25=1000. C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A can do a work in 21 days and B in 42 days. If they work on it together for 7 days, then what fraction of work is left?
questions.explanation: A will do in 7 days =(7/21)=1/3. B will do in 7 days =(7/42)=1/6. So, work left =(1-1/3-1/6)=3/6=1/2. D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A can paint a house in 55 days and B can do it in 66 days. Along with C, they did the job in 12 days only. Then, C alone can do the job in how many days?
questions.explanation: According to question, (1/55+1/66+1/c)=1/12. or, 1/c=(1/12)-(1/55)-(1/66)= (55-12-10)/660=(1/20). Ao, C will do in 20 days . D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A, B and C together can finish a task in 12 days. A is twice as productive as B and C alone can do the task in 36 days. In how many days can A and B do the task if C goes on leave?
questions.explanation: Let say, B can do in x days . So, A can do in (x/2) days. So, (1/x+2/x+1/36)=1/12. or, (1/x+2/x)=(1/12-1/36)=1/18. So, A and B can do the full work in 18 days. D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A, B and C can together do a job in 9 days. C alone can do the job in 36 days. In how many days can A and B do 50% of the job working together?
questions.explanation: A,B and C together can do in 1 day (1/9) part of the job . So, A and B can do in 1 day (1/9-1/36)=(1/12) part of the job. So, they together can do 50% of the job in (12/2)= 6 days. A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Giving two successive discounts of 25% is equal to giving one discount of ________%.
questions.explanation: Resultant discount is =(1-0.75×0.75)×100 =43.75 %. A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If a watch is being sold at Rs 7,225 which is marked at Rs 8,500, then what is the discount (in %) at which the watch is being sold?

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: On a machine there is 10% trade discount on the marked price of Rs 2,50,000. But the machine is sold at Rs 2,16,000 after giving a cash discount. How much is this cash discount (in %)?
questions.explanation: After trade discount price became=0.90×250000=225000 Rs. let say he gave a x Rs of cash discount. So, 2250000-x=216000. So, x=9000. So, percentage of cash discount was = (9000/225000)×100=4 %. B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A trader marks up his goods by 120% and offers 30% discount. What will be the selling price (in Rs) if the cost price is Rs 750?
questions.explanation: According to question, S.P=750×2.20×0.70=1155 Rs. D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Sanjay's test marks in two subjects, English and Hindi are in the ratio 7 : 11. If he got 20 marks more in Hindi than in English, what are his marks in English?
questions.explanation: Let say,he got in English 7k and in Hindi 11k. So, 11k=7k+20. or, 4k=20. or, k=5. So, he got 7×5=35 in English. A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: The ratio of present ages of Simi and Seema is 5 : 4. After 9 years the ratio of their ages will be 8 : 7. What is Simi's present age (in years)?
questions.explanation: Let say, Simi and Seema's present age is 5k and 4k. So, (5k+9)/(4k+9)=8/7 . or, 35k+63=32k+72 . or, 3k=9. or, k=3 . Simi's present age=5×3=15. B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Find the third proportional to 6 and 12.
questions.explanation: Let the third proportional is X. So, (6/12)=(12/x) or, x=12×12/6=24. So, C is correct choice. 7   5

questions.question: According to the will the wealth of Rs 21,25,000 was to be divided between the son and the daughter in the ratio   6 : 3 . How much did the son get (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: Son:Daughter=(7/6):(5/3)=7:10. So, son will get= 2125000×7/17=875000. A is correct choice. 1   1    1

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If Rs 25,000 is to be divided between A, B and C in the ratio 10 : 6 : 15 , then how much will C get (in Rs)?

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Rizwan has a box in which he kept red and blue marbles. The red marbles and blue marbles were in the ratio 5 : 4. After he lost 5 red marbles the ratio became 10 : 9. How many marbles does he have now?
questions.explanation: Red marble/Blue marble=5/4 . Let say ,he has 5k red marbles and 4k blue marbles . So, Now , (5k-5)/4k=10/9 . or, 45k-45=40k. or, k=9 . Now he has = (5k+4k-5)=(45+36-5)=76. C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: The average weight of L, M and N is 93 kg. If the average weight of L and M be 89 kg and that of M and N be 96.5 kg, then the weight (in kg) of M is ________.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Mahesh buys 3 shirts at an average price of Rs 1250. If he buys 2 more shirts at an average price of Rs 1450 what will be the average price (in Rs) of all the 5 shirts he buys?
questions.explanation: 1250×3+1450×2 Average price of 5 shirts =         5       = 1330 Rs. B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: In a one day match of 50 overs in an innings the Team A had a run rate of 6.1 runs per over. Team B is playing and 10 overs are left and the required run rate to tie the match is 6.5 per over. What is Team B's score now?
questions.explanation: B requires 50 × 6.1 = 305 runs to win . Let say, first 40 overs they had x as run rate . So, 40x + 10 × 6.5 = 305 or, 40x = 240 . So, B's score now =240 . C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Average of all even numbers between 222 and 250 is __________.
questions.explanation: There are total 15 even numbers present between 222 and 250 . Total of them = 2 (222 + 250) = 3540 . So, Average of them = 15        = 236 . C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A vendor buys bananas at 7 for Rs 6 and sells at 6 for Rs 7. What will be the result?
questions.explanation: C.P= 7 . S.P= 6 . 7       6 ( 6 −6 7 ) So, Gain=          7       × 100 = 36.10 % C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A miner sells a diamond to a trader at a profit of 40% and the trader sells it to a customer at a profit of 25%. If the customer pays Rs 56 lakhs to buy the diamond, what had it cost the miner (in Rs lakhs)?
questions.explanation: C.P = 1.25×1.40 = 32 lakh Rs. D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A grocer had 1600 kgs of wheat. He sold a part of it at 20% profit and the rest at 12% profit, so that he made a total profit of 17%. How much wheat (in kg) did he sell at 20% profit?
questions.explanation: Let the cost price of per kg wheat is x Rs. and he sells y kg at 20% profit. So, y × x × 0.20 + (1600 − y) × x × 0.12 = 0.17 × 1600 × x or, 0.20y + (192 − 0.12y) = 272 . 272−192 or, y =       0.08  = 1000 . B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A used two-wheeler dealer sells a scooter for Rs 46,000 and makes some loss. If he had sold it for Rs 58,000 his profit would have been double his loss. What was the cost price (in Rs) of the scooter?
questions.explanation: Let the cost price is x Rs. So, 58000 − x = 2 × (x − 46000) or, 58000 − x = 2x − 92000 or, 3x = 58000 + 92000 or, x = 50000 . D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: When a number is increased by 24, it becomes 115% of itself. What is the number?
questions.explanation: x + 24 = 1.15x . or, x = 0.15 = 160. A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Two numbers are 40% and 80% lesser than a third number. By how much percent is the second number to be enhanced to make it equal to the first number?
questions.explanation: Let the third no. be x Then , 1st no. = x - 40x/100 = 60x/100 2nd no. = x - 80x/100 = 20x/100 1st no. : 2nd no. = 60x/100 : 20x /100 => 3:1 Required % = ( 2/1) × 100 = 200% D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Price of diesel increased from Rs 45/litre to Rs 50/litre. How much should the consumption of diesel be reduced (in %) so as to increase expenditure by only 5%?
questions.explanation: Expenditure (E) = Consumption (C) × Price (P) Before the price increased, E = C 1 × 45 ----(1) After the price increased, Expenditure increased by 5% = 1.05E 1.05E = C 2 × 50 ----(2) On dividing equation (1) by (2) ⇒ 1/1.05 = (C 1/C2) × 45/50 ⇒ C 1/C2 = 200/189 Required cut in consumption = (200 -189)/200 × 100 ⇒ Required cut in consumption = 11/2 = 5.5% A is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A plane flies a distance of 1800 km in 5 hours. What is its average speed in meters/second?
questions.explanation: 1800 km  1800×1000 m        m Average speed=           5   h = 5×3600 sec = 100 sec . D is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If a boat goes upstream at a speed of 24 km/hr and comes back the same distance at 40 km/hr. What is the average speed (in km/hr) for the total journey.
questions.explanation: 2× Dis tan ce Dis tan ce  Dis tan ce       2×Dis tan ce×120       km Average speed=        24      + 40          =    (5+3 )Dis tan ce = 30 h . B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Two bikers A and B start and ride at 75 km/hr and 60 km/hr respectively towards each other. They meet after 20 minutes. How far (in km) were they from each other when they started?
questions.explanation: 75                60 In 20 min they will go total = 60 × 20 + 60 × 20 = 25 + 20 = 45 km . B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: Excluding stoppages, the speed of a bus is 80 kmph and including stoppages, it is 60 kmph. For how many minutes does the bus stop per hour?
questions.explanation: in 1 hour bus run for 60 km including stoppage. So, Bus will run at 80 km/h speed for = 80 × 60 = 45 min . So, in 1 hour it will definitely stop for =60-45=15 mins . B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: In 2 years at simple interest the principal increases by 8%. What will be the compound interest earned (in Rs) on Rs 10 lakhs in 2 years at the same rate?
questions.explanation: According to question , 2r P (1 + 100 ) = 1.08P or, r = 4 % . So, C.I = {10 (1.04)       − 10} lakh = 81600 Rs. B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: If the compound interest for the 3rd and 4th year on a certain principal is Rs 125 and Rs 135 respectively, what is the rate of interest (in %)?
questions.explanation: Interest for 4- years= ₹ 135 Interest for 3- years= ₹ 125. Therefore interest for ₹ 125 for 1- year =₹10 So interest for ₹ 100 for 1- year =(10/125) x100 = ₹8 Therefore rate of interest= 8% . C is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A certain bank offers 8% rate of interest on the 1st year and 9% on the 2nd year in a certain fixed deposit scheme. If Rs 17,658 are received after investing for 2 years in this scheme, then what was the amount (in Rs) invested?
questions.explanation: According to question, 17658 = P(1 + 8/100)(1 + 9/100) ⇒ 17658 = P × 108/100 × 109/100 ⇒ P = Rs. 15000 ∴ The amount invested = Rs. 15000 B is correct choice.

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: What is the difference (in Rs) in Compound interest earned in 1 year on a sum of Rs 25,000 at 20% per annum compounded semi-annually and annually?
questions.explanation: 10   1×2 Semi C.I = 25000 (1 + 100 )         − 25000 = 5250 Rs. 20   1 And, Annual C.I = 25000 (1 + 100 )       − 25000 = 5000 Rs. So, their difference is = 5250-5000= 250 Rs . B is correct choice.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a business transaction?
options: XYZ Ltd. is negotiating with the customer regarding the supply of goods.
XYZ Ltd. paid ₹10,000 for buying furniture for the shop.

questions.explanation: Given that x tan $$60^\circ$$ + cos $$45^\circ$$ = sec $$45^\circ$$ We know that value of tan $$60^\circ$$ = $$ \sqrt{3}$$, cos $$45^\circ$$ = $$\frac {1}{\sqrt{2}}$$ and sec $$45^\circ$$ = $$\sqrt{2}$$ => x$$ \sqrt{3}$$+ $$\frac {1}{\sqrt{2}}$$ = $$\sqrt{2}$$ => x$$ \sqrt{6}$$ + 1= 2 => x$$ \sqrt{6}$$ = 1 => x= $$ \frac{1}{\sqrt{6}}$$ Therefore $$ x^2 + 1 $$ = $$\frac{1} {6} $$ + 1 = $$ \frac{7}{6} $$

questions.question: An aeroplane flying horizontally at a height of 3 Km. above the ground is observed at a certain point on earth to subtend an angle of 60°. After 15 sec flight, its angle of elevation is changed to 30°. The speed of the aeroplane (taking √3 = 1.732) is
questions.explanation: AB = CD = 3000 m A and C are positions of aeroplane. $$\angle AOB$$ $$\angle AOB = 60\degree        \angle COD = 30\degree$$ In $$\triangle OAB$$, $$\tan 60\degree = \frac{AB}{OB}$$ => $$\sqrt{3} = \frac{3000}{OB}$$ =>OB = $$\frac{3000}{\sqrt{3}} = 1000\sqrt{3}$$ In $$\triangle OCD$$, $$\tan 30\degree = \frac{CD}{OD}$$ => $$\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} = \frac{3000}{OD}$$ =>CD = $$3000\sqrt{3} = 3000\sqrt{3}$$ BD = $$3000\sqrt{3} - 1000\sqrt{3} = 2000\sqrt{3}$$ Speed of aeroplane =$$\frac{2000\sqrt{3}}{15}$$ = 230.93 So, the answer would be option b)230.93 m/sec.

questions.explanation: Sum of digits at odd places is 7+8+6=21. Sum of digits at even places is 3+2A. Their difference = 21-(3+2A) = 18-2A The number will be divisible by 11 if 18-2A is 11 or 0. But to get to 11, 2A needs to be 7 where it will give a fraction as a digit. So 18-2A = 0. hence A = 9

questions.subject: quantitative_aptitude
questions.question: A can do a piece of work in 24 days, B in 32 days and C in 64 days. All being to do it together, but A leaves after 6 days and B leaves 6 days before the completion of the work. How many days did the work last?
questions.explanation: Number of days to complete the work A = 24 days B = 32 days C = 64 days. All worked together for 6 days,the fraction of the work that has been done is 6/24 = 1/4 by A 6/32 = 3/16 by B 6/64 = 3/32 by C. Total fraction of work done in First 6 days = 1/4 + 3/16 + 3/32 =(8 + 6 + 3) ÷32 = 17/32. Fraction of work left = 1 -17/32 = 15/32. Since B leaves 6 days before the completion of the work,it means that C works alone for 6 days. The fraction of work C does alone in those 6 days is 6/64 = 3/32. Therefore fraction of work done before B's departure = 15/32 - 3/32 = 12/32 =3/8. Let X be the number of days in which B and C did 3/8 of the work. Therefore, x/32 is done by B and x/64 is done by C. This shows that x/32 + x/64 = 3÷8 2x + x = 3 × 8 3x = 24 x = 24÷3 = 8 days. Total number of days = 6 + 8 + 6 = 20 days So, the answer would be option b)20. (0.75)3

questions.explanation: total runs scored by the player in 40 innings = 40 × 50 = 2000 total runs scored by the player in 38 innings after excluding 2 innings = 38 × 48 = 1824 sum of the scores of the excluded innings = 2000 - 1824 = 176 given that scores of the excluded innings differ by 172 hence lets take the highest score as x+172 and lowest score as x now x+x+172 = 176 2x = 4 x=2 highest score = 172+2 = 174

questions.explanation: ratio of gold and copper in A = 7 : 2 gold in A = 9 copper in A = 9 ratio of gold and copper in B = 7 : 11 gold in B = 18 copper in B = 18 If equal quantities of the alloys are melted to form a third alloy C 7        7        21 gold in C = 9 + 18 = 18 2        11        15 copper in C = 9 + 18 = 18 ratio of gold and copper in C = 21 : 15 = 7 : 5
